user_query:
I'm preparing a small local "internship portal" pilot for our partnership with River Town University, and I need the workspace configured with a reproducible deployment file plus an updated README that includes data-driven summaries. Please use only the files in this repo and produce the following deterministic changes and outputs:

Inputs to use:
- config/deployment.yaml
- data/internships.csv
- data/applications.csv
- docs/README.md (edit in place)

1) Compute summary metrics from the CSVs:
- For each department, calculate: total_internships; avg_stipend (rounded to 2 decimals); total_applications; accepted_applications. The stipend average is over internships (not applications). total_applications and accepted_applications are derived by joining applications to internships by internship_id.
- Overall totals: total_departments, total_internships, total_applications.

2) Write structured result files:
- output/summary/department_metrics.json with this structure:
  {
    "generated_from": {"internships_csv": "data/internships.csv", "applications_csv": "data/applications.csv"},
    "by_department": [
      {"department": "...", "total_internships": n, "avg_stipend": x.xx, "total_applications": n, "accepted_applications": n},
      ...
    ],
    "overall": {"total_departments": n, "total_internships": n, "total_applications": n}
  }
- output/summary/internship_application_counts.csv with columns exactly: internship_id,department,title,applications_count,accepted_count (one row per internship).

3) Edit docs/README.md in place, replacing only the marked blocks:
- Between <!-- SUMMARY_START --> and <!-- SUMMARY_END -->, insert a compact list where each line begins with "- Department:" and includes exactly these key-value pairs separated by " | " in this order: Department, Internships, Avg stipend (2 decimals), Applications, Accepted. After the per-department lines, add one final line starting with "Overall Totals:" that includes total_departments, total_internships, and total_applications in the same "key: value" style. Do not alter any content outside the markers.
- Between <!-- DEPLOYMENT_START --> and <!-- DEPLOYMENT_END -->, insert deployment notes that reflect config/deployment.yaml and the generated compose file. Include lines with:
  - Service name: <service_name>
  - Environment: <environment>
  - Image: <image>
  - Ports: <host_port>:<container_port>
  - Compose file: deploy/docker-compose.yml
  - Content dir (host): ./<volume_content_dir>
  - Container web root: /usr/share/nginx/html
  Also include a one-line example command to start it: docker compose -f deploy/docker-compose.yml up -d

4) Generate deploy/docker-compose.yml from config/deployment.yaml with these requirements:
- Top-level key version must be set to the YAML field compose_version.
- Under services, define a single service whose key equals service_name.
- That service must include: image (from image); ports with exactly one mapping "<host_port>:<container_port>"; environment mapping from env_vars; volumes with exactly one mapping "./<volume_content_dir>:/usr/share/nginx/html:ro".
- Create the deploy/ directory if it doesn’t exist.

General constraints:
- Do not fetch anything from the Internet; rely only on provided local files.
- Ensure all specified output paths exist and files are written with UTF-8 text encodings.
- Keep the README edits confined to the marked regions.

Explicit input paths:
- config/deployment.yaml
- data/internships.csv
- data/applications.csv
- docs/README.md

Explicit output paths to create:
- deploy/docker-compose.yml
- output/summary/department_metrics.json
- output/summary/internship_application_counts.csv
- docs/README.md (modified in place) All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
